compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
3183 (change 580, commit da27c5f30) delivered the merge-gate + snapshot ledger with a
divergent schema and verdict set vs. the APPROVED epic design (AC8/AC9 — the two ACs the
epic completion-verifier correctly FAILED on). This story reworks commit 7 to the approved
design AND performs the consumer cutover deferred from TS1 (in-toto payload) and TS2
(commit-ancestry era) — the write-seam signing, the merge-gate, and the ledger all live in
THIS commit. Depends on TS1 and TS2. (User rejected the delivered design; see d7c0.)

## Stack scoping (why the cutover is here + why TS2+TS3 land together)
This story AMENDS Gerrit change 580 = commit da27c5f30 (3183): `_seam.py`'s write-time
signing, `verify_authorship.py` (merge-gate), `compact.py`'s ledger, and `_state.py`'s
authorship summary. At this commit those consumers currently still call the epoch API that
TS2 REMOVED (`epoch_for_position`, `verify_authorship_at_epoch`), so the commit-7 patchset
is RED until this story rewires them — TS2+TS3 land together. `ensure_identity_for` (aliased
by `create_placeholder`) lives in commit 6, so `create_placeholder` lands here too.

## What

### 1. Write-seam in-toto cutover (`_seam.py::_apply_authorship`)
Stamp `author_sig` by calling `authorship.sign_event_authorship(event, signing_key,
principal=author_id)` (the in-toto Statement envelope) instead of
`sign_authorship(canonical_bytes, ...)`.

### 2. Merge-gate rewire (`verify_authorship.py`)
- Refactor `_ScopedEvent`: REPLACE the `epoch: int | None` field with `commit_sha: str |
  None` and `position: str | None` (the `{timestamp}-{uuid}` string). Live events populate
  `position` from the filename and resolve `commit_sha` lazily; ledger entries populate both
  from the ledger record (below).
- LIVE-event content binding: parse the DSSE payload as an in-toto Statement and check
  `subject[0].name == ev['uuid']` AND `subject[0].digest.sha256 ==
  authorship.authorship_content_hash(ev)` (replacing the raw-bytes `envelope.payload !=
  ev.payload` compare). Resolve the event's commit via
  `authorship.resolve_event_commit(position, ticket_dir, repo_root)`.
- Verify via `authorship.verify_authorship_at_commit(envelope, author_id, commit_sha,
  position, repo_root=...)` (replacing `epoch_for_position` + `verify_authorship_at_epoch`).
- **Four-way classification (the AC9 distinction):**
  - `unsigned` — no `author_sig`.
  - `unknown-author` — no `author_id` / not an identity ticket.
  - `verified` — content binds AND `verify_authorship_at_commit` verified (key valid at the
    event's commit).
  - `key_not_valid_at_era` — content binds AND the signature is cryptographically valid
    against SOME key in the identity's keyring (any era) BUT not one valid at the event's
    commit. Decided by a NEW helper `authorship.verify_authorship_any_key(envelope,
    identity_id, *, repo_root) -> registry.Verdict` that builds the trust root from ALL
    keyring public keys (regardless of validity window) and runs ONE `registry.verify`
    (boolean). valid-any-key AND NOT valid-at-commit ⇒ `key_not_valid_at_era`.
  - `bad-signature` — content mismatch, malformed envelope, or verifies against NO key the
    identity ever held (forged).
- Add the verdict constant `KEY_NOT_VALID_AT_ERA = "key_not_valid_at_era"` and extend the
  CLI `counts` dict (currently hardcoded `{VERIFIED, UNSIGNED, UNKNOWN_AUTHOR,
  BAD_SIGNATURE}`) with the new key so it is tallied and printed. Document the display
  grouping in the module docstring: `verified` / `unverified` (= `bad-signature` |
  `key_not_valid_at_era` | `unknown-author`) / `unsigned`. When `resolve_event_commit` fails
  for a LIVE event (unresolvable commit), the fail-closed verify yields non-`verified` and
  the event is reported like any other non-verified event.

## Migration note (unlanded + atomic)
The `authorship_ledger` wire format changes from `{event_uuid, author_id, author_sig,
epoch}` to `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey, position}`. The stack is
UNLANDED and lands atomically, so no real store holds an old-format ledger; there is no
in-place snapshot migration to write (a pre-feature snapshot with no ledger is unaffected;
the reducer cache version bump from TS2 already invalidates stale reductions).

### 3. Ledger (`compact.py::_build_authorship_ledger`)
Record `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey, position}` per signed folded
event, where:
- `content_hash` = `authorship.authorship_content_hash(ev)` (TS1 digest).
- `signature` = the event's `author_sig` (the DSSE Statement envelope).
- `signer_pubkey` = the actual public key that verified the signature. Because SSHSIG verify
  (`ssh-keygen -Y verify`) returns only pass/fail (NOT which key matched), determine it via a
  new `authorship.identify_signer(envelope, identity_id, *, repo_root) -> str | None` that
  iterates the identity's FULL keyring — ALL public keys, including REVOKED ones (so a valid
  signature by a since-revoked key is still identified, enabling the later
  `key_not_valid_at_era` classification) — verifying the envelope against each single-key
  trust root and returning the first that passes. **Single None-policy:** if `identify_signer`
  returns `None` (the signature verifies against NO key the identity ever held — a forged /
  foreign signature), STILL record a ledger entry (never omit — omitting would hide a forged
  event from the post-compaction merge-gate) with `signer_pubkey: null`; ledger verification
  (Section 4) classifies a null-`signer_pubkey` entry as `bad-signature`. The record always
  has exactly the five keys; `signer_pubkey` is a string or `null`.
- `position` = `{"commit_sha": <resolve_event_commit(...)>, "position": "<{ts}-{uuid}>"}` —
  the `position` string is the SAME `{timestamp}-{uuid}` value `verify_authorship_at_commit`
  already consumes for intra-commit ordering (NO separate integer index; the string ordering
  is the intra-commit index). `commit_sha` is resolved at compaction time.

### 4. Ledger verification (`verify_authorship.py::_ledger_events` + `_verify_signed`)
Rewrite `_ledger_events` to read the NEW fields (`content_hash`, `signature`,
`signer_pubkey`, `position.commit_sha`, `position.position`) — not the old
`author_id`/`author_sig`/`epoch`. For a ledger entry, verification is self-contained:
crypto-verify the `signature` envelope against a ONE-key trust root built from
`signer_pubkey`, check the Statement binds `content_hash`, and decide era from
`position.commit_sha` against the SURVIVING snapshot keyring (retired KEY files folded into
the same snapshot; `resolve_event_commit` still finds a retired file's ADD commit via `git
log --diff-filter=A` across history) via `verify_authorship_at_commit`'s ancestry logic —
emitting the same four-way verdict. A ledger entry with `signer_pubkey: null` is classified
`bad-signature` directly (no key ever verified it). No raw event file needed.

### 5. Verdict vocabulary (NOT the verify-signature schema)
`verify_signature_result.schema.json` is the schema for the SEPARATE `rebar verify-signature`
command (HMAC attestations: `unsigned|foreign_key|certified|mismatch`) — it is NOT touched.
The `verify-authorship` verdicts are its own vocabulary (string constants in
`verify_authorship.py`): `verified`, `unsigned`, `unknown-author`, `bad-signature`, and the
NEW `key_not_valid_at_era`, with the display grouping documented in that module. (The epic
AC's phrase "existing verify_signature_result verdicts" is read as the verdict-vocabulary
style, not a literal edit to that unrelated command's schema — reconciled per plan-review.)

### 6. `create_placeholder`
Add `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name, *, repo_root=None)` to
`identity.py` as a thin idempotent alias over the delivered `ensure_identity_for`, and
re-export it through the library facade chain so `rebar.create_placeholder(...)` is
importable: add it to `_lib_writes.py` and to `rebar/__init__.py`'s `__all__`/exports
alongside `ensure_identity_for`.

## Scope
In: the write-seam in-toto cutover, the merge-gate rewire to commit-ancestry + in-toto
content-binding + the four-way `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict, the approved ledger schema +
self-contained ledger verification, the `signer_pubkey`/`identify_signer` +
`verify_authorship_any_key` helpers, and the `create_placeholder` alias + re-export. Out:
authorship payload machinery (TS1, done); era model machinery (TS2, done); docs/ADR (TS4).
NOTE: `verify_signature_result.schema.json` is deliberately NOT modified.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A live signed event round-trips: with `identity.signing_key` set and a current
  identity holding a valid key, an event written through the seam is classified `verified`
  by `rebar verify-authorship` (proves the in-toto `_seam` stamping + the merge-gate in-toto
  content-binding + commit-ancestry verify are wired end-to-end).
- [ ] The SNAPSHOT authorship ledger records `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature,
  signer_pubkey, position}` (position = `{commit_sha, position:"<{ts}-{uuid}>"}`) per signed
  folded event; a round-trip test signs events, compacts (horizon=0, threshold=1), and
  asserts `verify-authorship --all` returns `verified` from the ledger ALONE (raw event
  files retired). A test also asserts the ledger record has exactly those five keys and that
  `signer_pubkey` equals the actual signing key.
- [ ] `verify-authorship` emits a distinct `key_not_valid_at_era` for an event whose
  signature is cryptographically valid by a key the identity holds but which was NOT valid at
  the event's commit (e.g. an event signed by a since-revoked key), distinct from
  `bad-signature` and `unknown-author` (test drives the CLI and asserts the verdict string).
- [ ] `bad-signature` is still emitted for a forged signature (verifies against no key the
  identity ever held) and for a content-binding mismatch (test).
- [ ] The `verify-authorship` verdict vocabulary exposes `key_not_valid_at_era` (a test
  asserts the constant / that the CLI can print it) and the module documents the
  `verified/unverified/unsigned` display grouping; `verify_signature_result.schema.json` is
  unchanged (a test asserts its enum is still `unsigned|foreign_key|certified|mismatch`).
- [ ] `rebar.create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)` mints a keyless
  placeholder identity, is idempotent on repeat (returns the same identity), and is
  importable from the top-level `rebar` package (test).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py` (in-toto cutover: stamp author_sig via sign_event_authorship)
- `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (_ScopedEvent epoch->commit_sha/position; in-toto content-binding; verify_authorship_at_commit; key_not_valid_at_era four-way verdict; read new ledger)
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (approved ledger schema + signer_pubkey via per-key identify)
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` (verify_authorship_any_key + identify_signer helpers)
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (create_placeholder alias over ensure_identity_for)
- `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` (re-export create_placeholder)
- `src/rebar/__init__.py` (export create_placeholder for rebar.create_placeholder)

file_impact.reason: commit-ancestry + in-toto binding + key_not_valid_at_era + new ledger read

compiled_state.title: transition: snapshot ledger schema + key_not_valid_at_era verdict (approved AC8/AC9)
